data.title: Resolve real cargo package names in the scope-aware build-gate (and forbid --lib-only self-verification)
data.executiveSummary: The scope-aware build gate in src/build-runner.ts derives cargo `-p` flags from workspace DIRECTORY names (e.g. `crates/data/` → `data`) instead of real package names (e.g. `stockfan-data`), so `cargo build -p data` fails instantly with "package ID specification `data` did not match any packages" on any workspace that prefixes crate names — false-failing every gate attempt and aborting the run before anything compiles. Fix 1 adds a `cargo metadata`-backed `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` that maps dir→real package `name` (with a documented dir-name fallback and a never-throw + per-cwd cache), wired into `detectTouchedCargoPackages`; Fix 2 confirms the already-complete touched-set extraction now yields correct names; Fix 3 strengthens the implementation/QA prompt builders (in src/prompts.ts, invoked by src/stages/implementation.ts and verify.ts) to forbid `--lib`-only verification and require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` plus spec-mandated e2e/integration targets.

acceptanceCriteria.statement: src/build-runner.ts exports `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]` that runs `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml` (bounded by the existing timeout envelope, via discrete argv `spawnSync`, no `shell:true`), parses the JSON, and maps each touched `crates/<dir>/` segment to the workspace package whose `manifest_path` parent directory equals `crates/<dir>/` (compare the directory CONTAINING the package's Cargo.toml, supporting manifest-in-subdir), returning the real package `name`s deduped and in first-seen order.

AC-02 — Never-throw + fallback chain: the metadata helper is wrapped in a defensive try/catch that NEVER throws; on spawn error / non-zero exit / timeout / missing cargo / no match, it falls back to returning the input touched directory names verbatim (identity), so workspaces where dir==name and non-cargo dirs degrade to today's behavior identically. The fallback is documented in a JSDoc comment.

AC-03 — Per-cwd in-memory cache: a module-level `Map<cwd, MetadataResult>` memoizes the parsed `cargo metadata` output so a single gate run that calls the resolver multiple times spawns `cargo metadata` at most once per cwd. The cache is process-local (cleared on process exit; never persisted across runs to avoid stale results).

AC-04 — `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd, baseRef?)` keeps its existing `git diff --name-only` spawn + regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` + dedupe logic, but passes the collected directory segments through `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the FINAL step so it returns REAL package names; on the stockfan shape (dirs data/tools/workflows) it returns `['stockfan-data','stockfan-tools','stockfan-workflows']`, producing `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` end-to-end via the unchanged `scopedCargoArgs`/`scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs`.

AC-05 — Complete touched set: a touched `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` makes `stockfan-workflows` appear in the resolved scope (regex already captures the `workflows` segment; this AC asserts the dir→name mapping covers it, so spec-mandated integration/e2e tests in a touched crate run via `cargo test -p stockfan-workflows`).

AC-06 — New unit tests (hermetic, `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked) covering: (a) dir→name resolution against a stubbed `cargo metadata` JSON with prefixed names + a manifest-in-subdir package; (b) metadata failure (spawn error / non-zero exit / timeout) falls back to the dir-name identity list; (c) a cache-hit scenario where the metadata spawn mock is called only once across two resolver calls for the same cwd; (d) an end-to-end workspace where dir != name produces correct `-p` flags through `scopedCargoArgs`.

AC-07 — Fix 3 (prompt-level): the implementation/verify self-verification prompt builders (the authoritative text lives in src/prompts.ts — `buildImplementPrompt` and the QA/verify prompt invoked by src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts) are strengthened to REQUIRE `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` flag (so integration binaries under `tests/` run), PLUS any spec-mandated e2e/integration target, and to include an explicit instruction: do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence; `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries. This is a prompt-text change only — no control-flow or nodes/workflow/pipeline change.

AC-08 — No regression: all existing scope-aware tests pass unchanged (build-runner-touched-crates, build-runner-autoscope, build-runner-inscope-classification, build-runner-scoped-args, build-runner-packages, build-runner-nonregression, build-runner-timeout, build-runner-docs); non-cargo repos (go/python/node/mixed) and non-git dirs remain byte-identical (the metadata tier runs only when `language === 'rust'` and a non-empty scope resolves, exactly as today).

AC-09 — Theme method-binding preserved: any new rendering uses method-style `theme.fg(...)` (or a wrapper `const fg=(c,t)=>theme.fg(c,t)`); NEVER destructures `theme.fg`. No new theme tokens are introduced and the rendering layer is untouched. (tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts must still pass.)

AC-10 — `npm run typecheck` passes (strict-clean) and `npm test` passes (existing + new) with no new runtime dependencies; the only new spawned process is `cargo metadata --no-deps --manifest-path ...`, cached per cwd per run.

nonFunctional: Performance: `cargo metadata --no-deps` is the single new spawned process, bounded by the existing timeout envelope, and memoized per cwd so a gate run spawns it at most once; cache is process-local (never persisted) to avoid staleness across runs.

Reliability / fault tolerance: the metadata resolver and the gate itself NEVER throw — every failure path (spawn error, non-zero exit, timeout, missing cargo, malformed JSON, no matching package) degrades gracefully to the documented dir-name identity fallback, preserving today's safe `[]`/workspace-wide semantics; a non-cargo or non-git repo behaves identically to before.

Correctness / no-false-green: Fix 3 is prompt-only and must not change control flow; the `--lib` prohibition removes a vacuous-green vector (declaring success on unit tests only) without weakening the deterministic gate's out-of-scope classifier (which still never grants a false green).

Backward compatibility: dir==name workspaces get an identity mapping (no-op), so existing tests and real dir==name monorepos are byte-identical; the scoped-vs-workspace-wide gate STRATEGY is unchanged (touched-crate scoping remains default).

Security: package names flow as discrete argv elements through `spawnSync` with no `shell:true` (matching the existing spawn pattern in detectTouchedCargoPackages/runBuildGate), so no metadata-derived string ever reaches a shell.

TypeScript strict-clean + zero new runtime dependencies; changes confined to src/build-runner.ts (Fix 1+2) and prompt text in src/prompts.ts (Fix 3) plus new tests — nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts and the rendering layer are untouched.
openQuestions: Fix 3 location: the authoritative prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts (buildImplementPrompt + the QA/verify prompt), which the stages merely call — not inline in src/stages/implementation.ts/verify.ts as the task brief implied. Confirm the `--lib` prohibition should be added in src/prompts.ts (recommended: it is the single source of truth the stages consume) rather than re-stated in each stage.

Fix 2 scope: the existing regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` ALREADY captures every touched crate segment (including `workflows` from `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`), so no extraction fix is required — Fix 2 is fully delivered by Fix 1's dir→name conversion. Confirm there is no separate single-crate edge case to handle (e.g. root-level workspace Cargo.toml edits touching ALL packages).

Should `resolveCargoPackageNames` also be exported for unit testing even if `detectTouchedCargoPackages` is the only internal caller? (Recommended: yes, export it so AC-06 tests can target it directly without going through git diff.)
Cache invalidation: confirm a process-local Map is acceptable (recommended: yes — a gate run is a single process and cross-run staleness is the real risk); no TTL or fs-based cache is needed.

Metadata matching granularity: confirm matching on the directory CONTAINING the package's Cargo.toml (`manifest_path` parent dir) vs the package `name` itself — recommended to match directory, since the bug is precisely that directory != name and the mapping must bridge them.

data.title: Cargo Package Name Resolution for Scope-Aware Build Gate

scenarios.title: Resolver maps a touched crate directory to its real package name via workspace metadata
scenarios.given: a Rust workspace where the crate directory crates/data/ maps to the package stockfan-data according to its Cargo.toml manifest_path
scenarios.when: the package-name resolver is given the touched directory segment 'data' for that working directory
scenarios.then: it returns the real package name 'stockfan-data' derived by comparing the touched crates/<dir>/ path against each workspace package's manifest parent directory
andClauses: the metadata is obtained from cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml via discrete argv spawnSync with no shell:true
the returned names are deduped and in first-seen order

scenarios.title: Resolver matches a package whose manifest lives in a subdirectory
scenarios.given: a workspace package whose Cargo.toml resides in a subdirectory relative to its crate directory
scenarios.when: the resolver maps the corresponding touched directory to a package name
scenarios.then: it matches using the directory that contains the package's Cargo.toml and returns the correct package name

scenarios.title: Metadata failure degrades to the directory-name identity list instead of throwing
scenarios.given: cargo metadata fails to run for any reason (spawn error, non-zero exit, timeout, missing cargo, or malformed JSON)
scenarios.when: the resolver is invoked for that working directory
scenarios.then: it returns the input touched directory names verbatim as an identity list
andClauses: the resolver never throws under any failure path
the fallback behavior is documented in a JSDoc comment
a simple dir==name workspace and a non-cargo dir degrade to today's behavior identically

scenarios.title: A touched directory with no matching package falls back safely
scenarios.given: a touched directory segment that has no corresponding package in the workspace metadata
scenarios.when: the resolver cannot find a matching package for that directory
scenarios.then: it degrades to returning that directory's name without raising an error

scenarios.title: A repeated resolver call for the same working directory reuses the cached metadata
scenarios.given: the resolver has already run cargo metadata for a given working directory within the current process
scenarios.when: the resolver is invoked again for that same working directory
scenarios.then: the cached metadata result is reused and cargo metadata is not spawned a second time
andClauses: a single gate run that calls the resolver multiple times spawns cargo metadata at most once per cwd

scenarios.title: The cache is process-local and never persists across runs
scenarios.given: a previous run that populated the metadata cache
scenarios.when: a new run starts in a fresh process
scenarios.then: the cache does not carry over and the metadata is re-resolved fresh to avoid stale results

data.title: Scope-aware cargo build-gate: resolve REAL package names via `cargo metadata` (deep-research / open-issues gate)
data.summary: The build gate's `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:161) maps `git diff` paths `crates/<seg>/` to the DIRECTORY name `<seg>` and `scopedCargoArgs` (:213) passes those verbatim as `-p <name>`, so any workspace that prefixes crate names (stockfan: dir `data` → package `stockfan-data`) makes cargo abort with "package ID specification `data` did not match any packages" BEFORE compiling — a framework-derived false fail the repair loop cannot fix. The single authoritative fix is to map each touched dir → the workspace package whose Cargo.toml directory matches it, using Cargo's OWN introspection. **Best practice (BP-1):** `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` (SRC-1, doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/commands/cargo-metadata.html) is the Cargo-recommended, network-free, deterministic way to enumerate a workspace; its JSON exposes `workspace_root`, `workspace_default_members`, and `packages[].name` + `packages[].manifest_path` (absolute). **BP-2:** pin `--format-version 1` (the `format-version` field exists precisely so consumers pin and avoid breakage). **BP-3:** `--no-deps` keeps it fast and deterministic (no dep-graph resolution, no network). **BP-4:** follow the repo's own never-throw convention — `detectTouchedCargoPackages` already swallows every error into `[]`; the new `resolveCargoPackageNames` must reuse the established `{ cwd, timeout: timeoutMs, encoding:"utf8" }` spawn envelope (build-runner.ts:~581) with discrete argv (no `shell:true`) and degrade to the dir-name identity list (today's behavior) on spawn error / non-zero exit / timeout / missing cargo / no match. **BP-5:** memoize the parsed metadata in a module-level `Map<cwd, MetadataResult>` (process-local; a gate run calls the resolver multiple times and metadata is not free). **BP-6 (Fix 3):** `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` (SRC-2, cargo test Test Selection) — `--lib` restricts to `src/` unit tests and silently skips the `tests/` integration binaries; omitting the filter runs lib + integration targets. The authoritative prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts (`buildImplementPrompt`:93, `buildQaPrompt`:97), invoked from src/stages/implementation.ts:10/:73 and verify.ts — both need an explicit "do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence; require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec-mandated e2e/integration" instruction. All four prior research issues are RESOLVED and carry forward as implementation guidance, so there are NO open research blockers.

options.name: Option 1 (RECOMMENDED): `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps` dir→name resolver + per-cwd cache + never-throw dir-name fallback
options.tradeoffs: Authoritative: Cargo's own introspection resolves real package names and handles prefixed names, manifest-in-subdir, workspace `name.workspace = true` inheritance, and path-deps correctly (SRC-1). Network-free and deterministic via `--no-deps`; schema-pinned via `--format-version 1` (BP-2/BP-3). Adds exactly ONE cached spawn per cwd per gate run (BP-5); reuses the existing spawnSync timeout envelope (build-runner.ts:~581) with discrete argv so package/path data never reach a shell. ACCEPTED DEGRADATION (prior ISS-3, now closed): a half-broken target-repo manifest makes `cargo metadata` exit non-zero → the never-throw fallback returns the dir-name identity list for THAT repo only (= byte-identical today's behavior); document this in a JSDoc on `resolveCargoPackageNames`. Wire it as the FINAL step of `detectTouchedCargoPackages` so `scopedCargoArgs`/scopedBuild/Test/ClippyArgs (build-runner.ts:213+) need NO change and the whole `-p` flag family is fixed end-to-end. No new runtime deps.

options.name: Option 2: Hand-parse each `crates/<dir>/Cargo.toml` to read `name =`
options.tradeoffs: No spawn, but fragile: workspace inheritance (`[workspace.package]` + `name.workspace = true`) forces you to resolve inheritance by hand or pull in a TOML parser dep (rejected — repo is strict and dep-free for this); also mishandles manifest-in-subdir and virtual manifests. Worse correctness/fragility tradeoff than Cargo's own metadata. Rejected.

options.name: Option 3: Abandon scoping — `cargo build/test/clippy --workspace`
options.tradeoffs: Always name-correct, but OUT OF SCOPE (task explicitly forbids changing the scoped-vs-workspace-wide gate strategy) and far slower on large workspaces — compiles/tests the entire workspace on every gate run, defeating the touched-crate scoping that is the current default. Rejected for strategy + perf reasons.

options.name: Option 4 (Fix 3 sub-decision, RECOMMENDED): Prompt-level verification enforcement in buildImplementPrompt + buildQaPrompt
options.tradeoffs: Strengthen the authoritative prompt builders (src/prompts.ts:93 and :97) to REQUIRE `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` flag plus any spec-mandated e2e/integration target, with an explicit 'do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence; `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries' instruction (SRC-2). Keeps the change within the 'do NOT touch nodes/workflow/pipeline/render' constraint (prompt-text only, no control-flow change). Relies on the LLM agent honoring the instruction — the same trust model the rest of the gate already uses. Heavier control-flow enforcement is out of scope. Also defensively strengthen buildQaPrompt even though verify.ts does not currently import it (prior implementer action item, now folded into AC-07).

data.title: Scope-aware cargo build-gate emits DIRECTORY names as `-p` flags instead of real package names
data.summary: The scope-aware cargo build-gate in src/build-runner.ts false-fails on any Cargo workspace where a crate's directory name differs from its declared package name (e.g. dir `crates/data/` → package `stockfan-data`). `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (lines ~161-180) runs `git diff --merge-base main --name-only`, extracts the first `crates/<dir>/` segment of each path via regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//`, and returns those directory NAMES verbatim through `dedupePreservingOrder`. There is NO step that resolves directory→package name. `scopedCargoArgs` (line ~213) then passes each entry through as `-p <name>`, producing e.g. `cargo build -p data -p tools -p workflows --quiet`. Cargo's `-p` flag matches against the package `name` field from `Cargo.toml`, not paths, so it rejects `data` instantly with `error: package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages` (exit 101) — before compiling anything. The repair loop cannot fix it because the command is framework-derived and identical on every retry; the run aborts after 3 attempts + pre-merge. On the stockfan shape (dirs data/tools/workflows → real packages stockfan-data/stockfan-tools/stockfan-workflows/stockfan-api) the run 2026-07-20T06-16-52-090Z aborted for exactly this reason. A secondary issue: the implementation agent declared success after `cargo test -p stockfan-data --lib --quiet` (lib unit tests only), so even the correct names would have skipped integration binaries under `tests/` and spec-mandated e2e in `crates/workflows/tests/`. Fix: add `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` that runs cached `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml` (never-throw, dir-name fallback, per-cwd memoization) and wire it as the final mapping step in `detectTouchedCargoPackages`; strengthen `buildImplementPrompt`/`buildQaPrompt` in src/prompts.ts to forbid `--lib`-only verification.
hypotheses: L1-H1 [p=0.85, CONFIRMED] — The gate emits directory names as `-p` flags; cargo rejects them because the real package `name` differs. Evidence: src/build-runner.ts:171 regex captures `m[1]` (the `[^/]+` dir segment), line 180 returns `dedupePreservingOrder(pkgs)` directly with no metadata resolution; grep for `resolveCargoPackageNames`/`cargo metadata` in build-runner.ts returns nothing. Prediction: on the stockfan shape the gate argv contains `-p data` not `-p stockfan-data`.

L1-H2 [p=0.10, DISPROVEN] — The regex drops the `workflows` crate (e2e dir) so the touched set is incomplete. Evidence: regex `/(?:^|/)crates/([^/]+)//` against `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_foo.rs` captures `workflows` correctly (the `[^/]+` matches up to the next `/`). The e2e crate is captured; it is simply mis-NAMED as `-p workflows`. Fix 2 is transitively resolved by Fix 1's dir→name mapping — no regex change needed.

L1-H3 [p=0.05, DISPROVEN] — A spawn timeout or empty-scope fallback causes the failure. Evidence: the run log shows exit 101 originating FROM cargo (`package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages`), i.e. a non-empty but wrong argv reached cargo; an empty scope would have produced a workspace-wide command with no `-p` flags, which would not yield that exact cargo error.

L2-H1a [p=0.95, CONFIRMED] under L1-H1 — No `cargo metadata` resolution step exists; `detectTouchedCargoPackages` returns directory segments as-is. Evidence: full function body lines 161-180 has no spawn of `cargo metadata`, no JSON parse, no manifest lookup.

L2-H1b [p=0.05, DISPROVEN] under L1-H1 — Resolution exists but is wired incorrectly. Evidence: grep confirms neither `resolveCargoPackageNames` nor `cargo metadata` appears anywhere in build-runner.ts, so there is nothing to mis-wire.

L3-Fix3 [p=0.90, CONFIRMED] — Self-verification guidance is too weak: src/prompts.ts:93-98 (`buildImplementPrompt`) and :97-98 (`buildQaPrompt`) only say 'Run the full test suite and verify build succeeds' with no `--lib` prohibition and no explicit `cargo test -p <pkg>` requirement. The run log corroborates: the agent declared green on `cargo test -p stockfan-data --lib --quiet`.
data.rootCause: `src/build-runner.ts` `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (lines ~161-180) extracts the first `crates/<dir>/` path segment via regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` and returns those DIRECTORY names directly (`dedupePreservingOrder(pkgs)`) with NO resolution to real package `name`s. `scopedCargoArgs` (line ~213) emits each as `-p <dir-name>`. Cargo's `-p` flag matches the package `name` declared in `Cargo.toml` (`[package] name = "..."`), not the directory path, so any workspace that prefixes crate names (dir `data` → package `stockfan-data`) produces `cargo build -p data` → `error: package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages` (exit 101) before compilation. The repair loop cannot recover because the argv is framework-derived and identical every retry. EXACT LOCATIONS: (1) src/build-runner.ts:171 regex captures directory segment; (2) src/build-runner.ts:180 returns segments verbatim (the missing `resolveCargoPackageNames` call site); (3) src/build-runner.ts:213 `scopedCargoArgs` passes through as `-p`; (4) src/build-runner.ts:551 `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)` invocation feeds the gate. Secondary (Fix 3): src/prompts.ts:93-98 `buildImplementPrompt`/`buildQaPrompt` provide only generic 'Run the full test suite' guidance with no `--lib` prohibition, allowing the implementation agent to declare success on `cargo test -p <pkg> --lib` (lib unit tests only, skipping `tests/` integration binaries and `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`). RECOMMENDED FIX: (a) add `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` — discrete-argv `spawnSync('cargo',['metadata','--format-version','1','--no-deps','--manifest-path', path.join(cwd,'Cargo.toml')], {encoding:'utf8', timeout envelope})`, parse JSON, match each touched `crates/<dir>/` against the parent directory of each workspace package's `manifest_path`, return real `name`s deduped/first-seen-order; never-throw try/catch with dir-name identity fallback; module-level `Map<cwd, MetadataResult>` cache so one gate run spawns `cargo metadata` at most once per cwd; (b) insert `dirs = resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the final step before `dedupePreservingOrder` in `detectTouchedCargoPackages`; (c) no regex change — the existing capture already includes `workflows`; (d) strengthen `buildImplementPrompt`/`buildQaPrompt` text to REQUIRE `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` plus spec-mandated e2e, with an explicit 'do NOT declare green on --lib-only evidence' line. REGRESSION TESTS: hermetic tests stubbing `node:child_process.spawnSync` to (a) return a prefixed-name manifest JSON and assert dir→name mapping incl. manifest-in-subdir; (b) return spawn-error/non-zero/timeout and assert dir-name identity fallback; (c) call resolver twice for same cwd and assert spawn invoked once (cache hit); (d) end-to-end `scopedCargoArgs` on dir!=name shape produces `-p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows`. PREVENTION: the gate's job is package-scoped cargo invocation, so package IDs must always be sourced from `cargo metadata` (the source of truth for package names), never inferred from filesystem layout; a one-line guard asserting every emitted `-p` value exists in `cargo metadata`'s `packages[].name` would make any future regression fail loudly instead of silently passing dir names through.
reproductionSteps: Preconditions: a Cargo workspace (default members) where at least one member's directory name differs from its declared package name — e.g. stockfan: `crates/data/Cargo.toml` declares `name = "stockfan-data"`, `crates/tools/` → `stockfan-tools`, `crates/workflows/` → `stockfan-workflows`.

From a feature branch with changes under `crates/data/`, `crates/tools/`, and `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`, trigger the build gate (any SUPER_DEV run that reaches runBuildGate with `language === 'rust'` and a non-empty touched scope).

Watch the gate invoke `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd)`: it spawns `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base main --name-only`, regex-captures the first `crates/<seg>/` of each line → `['data','tools','workflows']`, and returns those DIRECTORY names with no `cargo metadata` lookup.

`spawnSync('cargo',['build','-p','data','-p','tools','-p','workflows','--quiet'])` exits 101 with stderr `error: package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages` — emitted BEFORE any compilation, on the first attempt.

The repair loop re-runs the identical framework-derived command (3 attempts + pre-merge, all byte-identical because the framework, not the agent, owns the `-p` flags); every attempt fails identically; the run aborts.

MINIMAL deterministic repro (no cargo/git needed): assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs(['data'])` deep-equals `['cargo','build','-p','data','--quiet']` — proving the gate passes the raw directory name straight through with no metadata resolution. The fix flips this to `-p stockfan-data` once `resolveCargoPackageNames` is wired in.

Secondary repro for Fix 3: in the run log, grep the implementation stage's tool calls — it declared `allTestsPass: true` after running only `cargo test -p stockfan-data --lib --quiet`; no `tests/` integration binary and no `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` target was ever executed, confirming the `--lib`-only green path the strengthened prompts must forbid.

data.title: Codebase Assessment — Cargo Package Name Resolution (build-runner gate + verify prompts)
data.summary: pi-super-dev is a pure-TypeScript pi-extension (strict, ESM, `type: module`) implementing a 13-stage development pipeline. There is NO running API/UI server — it is a library consumed by the `pi` CLI, so verification is `npm run typecheck` + `npm test` (vitest), not a live service. The target of this fix is `src/build-runner.ts`, the "deterministic build/test/typecheck gate — the HARD test oracle" that spawns real cargo/git commands and is consumed by Stages 9/9.2/11. The confirmed bug: `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:161-181) returns workspace DIRECTORY names (`crates/<seg>/` → `<seg>`), which `scopedCargoArgs` (src/build-runner.ts:213-228) passes verbatim as `-p <name>`; cargo rejects these on any workspace that prefixes crate names. Fix 1 adds a `cargo metadata --no-deps` resolver mapping dir→real-package-name; Fix 2 verifies the full touched set survives; Fix 3 is a prompt change. Key localization finding: the actual prompt TEXT does not live in `src/stages/implementation.ts`/`verify.ts` (they only orchestrate agents + append build-gate failure strings) — it lives in the pure prompt builders in `src/prompts.ts` (`buildImplementPrompt` ~line 95, `buildImplementationSummaryPrompt` ~line 98), so Fix 3 must edit `src/prompts.ts`, with the idiomatic injection point being the Language-Specific Instructions block (`li`/`lang`) already threaded into `buildImplementPrompt`. All three fixes are additive and fit existing patterns: the module already centralizes spawnSync behind a single import (src/build-runner.ts:18), stubs it in tests via `vi.mock("node:child_process")` (tests/build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts:33-37), and follows a strict never-throw safe-degradation + pure-helper convention. No new runtime deps, no server to bring up. SERVICES NOTE: this package has no API or UI server entrypoint — it is a CLI library (package.json scripts: build/typecheck/test only; no start/dev/serve, no PORT env, no health endpoint). The verify-loop should NOT attempt to bring up a service; gate green is determined solely by `npm run typecheck` (tsc --noEmit) and `npm test` (vitest run) both passing.

patterns.consistency: Strict and uniform across the module. Every spawn-based function (detectTouchedCargoPackages) is fully try/caught and returns a safe empty value; argv builders (scopedCargoArgs family, src/build-runner.ts:213-276) are pure & side-effect-free. The new `resolveCargoPackageNames` MUST follow both: never throw (degrade to dir-name fallback), and keep scopedCargoArgs pure.
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:18 (import), src/build-runner.ts:161-181 (`detectTouchedCargoPackages` wraps entire body in try/catch returning [] on any failure)
patterns.name: Side-effecting module: single `spawnSync` import, pure argv helpers, never-throw

patterns.consistency: Canonical and mandatory — real git/cargo must NEVER run in CI. New tests for `resolveCargoPackageNames` (cargo metadata stub, cache-hit, failure fallback) and the end-to-end `-p` flag test should reuse this exact mock pattern and assert discrete argv elements (named `cargo`, no `shell:true`).
patterns.example: tests/build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts:33-37 (`vi.mock("node:child_process", () => ({ spawnSync: vi.fn() }))`), tests/build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts:44,230 (`const spawn = spawnSync as ...; spawn.mockReturnValue({...}); spawn.mock.calls[0]?.[1])
patterns.name: spawnSync stubbing in tests via vi.mock — assert argv from mock.calls

patterns.consistency: Central architectural invariant. Fix 1/2 must slot `resolveCargoPackageNames` INSIDE `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (tier iii, rust-only) — do not add a new tier or change precedence. The git-diff spawn stays the only spawn in tier iii; cargo metadata becomes the second cached spawn.
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:540-554 (opts.testPackages → SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES → detectTouchedCargoPackages only when cmds0.language==='rust' → []), src/build-runner.ts:561-566 (shallow-copy cmds with scoped argv when rust + non-empty)
patterns.name: runBuildGate FOUR-tier precedence (opts > env > autodetect(rust-only) > workspace-wide)

patterns.consistency: Order-preserving dedupe is THE convention for package sets. The metadata resolver must dedupe + preserve first-seen order of touched dirs, and the cache must be keyed by cwd only (never across runs).
patterns.name: dedupePreservingOrder + first-seen order everywhere

patterns.consistency: Strong and uniform: stages orchestrate (call ctx.agent, append build-gate error strings src/stages/verify.ts:106,219) while ALL prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts as `[ctxBlock(s,c), '', '## Phase', ...].join('\n')`. Fix 3 (--lib ban + full cargo test -p <pkg> requirement) belongs in src/prompts.ts, ideally injected via the existing Language-Specific Instructions `lang`/`li` variable so cargo guidance is scoped to rust workspaces.
patterns.example: src/prompts.ts:95 (buildImplementPrompt — array.join with `li` Language-Specific block injection) and src/prompts.ts:98 (buildImplementationSummaryPrompt — 'Run the full test suite and verify build succeeds'), consumed in src/stages/implementation.ts:10,73-77,122
patterns.name: Prompt builders are PURE string-array.join functions in src/prompts.ts (NOT in stages/)

patterns.consistency: Every exported function carries rich JSDoc citing the exact test scenarios that pin its contract and explicit safe-degradation guarantees. New `resolveCargoPackageNames` export should match this: document the cargo metadata mapping, the dir-name fallback chain, the per-cwd cache, and reference the new test scenarios.
patterns.example: src/build-runner.ts:30-86 (DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS doc referencing SCENARIO-007/006/008/014/020/022/023), src/build-runner.ts:140-160 (detectTouchedCargoPackages doc)
patterns.name: JSDoc-heavy exports with SCENARIO-NNNN test cross-references and 'NEVER throws / byte-identical' guarantees

recommendations: Implement Fix 1 inside `src/build-runner.ts` exactly as the patterns dictate: add `export function resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd: string, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]` that spawns `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml` via the module's existing single `spawnSync` import (src/build-runner.ts:18), with the SAME bounded-timeout + `encoding:'utf8'` envelope, parses JSON, maps each touched `crates/<dir>/` to the workspace package whose `manifest_path` parent dir matches `crates/<dir>/`, and returns dedupePreservingOrder'd real names (src/build-runner.ts:99). Wire it as the final mapping step at the end of `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:179) BEFORE the dedupe return — pass the collected dir segments through it. Add a per-cwd in-process Map cache keyed by cwd holding the parsed metadata (spawn at most once per cwd per run).

Honor the never-throw + fallback invariant explicitly: if cargo metadata fails / times out / cargo not installed / matches nothing, `resolveCargoPackageNames` MUST return the touchedDirs unchanged (current dir-name behavior), wrapped in try/catch — this is the same safe-degradation contract as `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts:140-160). This guarantees backward compatibility (dir==name workspaces, non-cargo/non-git repos) and acceptance criterion #7.

For Fix 3, edit `src/prompts.ts` — NOT src/stages/implementation.ts or verify.ts (those only orchestrate). The `--lib` ban + full `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec-mandated e2e requirement belongs in `buildImplementPrompt` (src/prompts.ts:95) and/or `buildImplementationSummaryPrompt` (src/prompts.ts:98), and the idiomatic injection point is the existing Language-Specific Instructions variable `li`/`lang` already joined into buildImplementPrompt (so cargo guidance is gated to rust workspaces only, matching the gate's `cmds0.language === 'rust'` guard at src/build-runner.ts:550,561).

Write tests in `tests/build-runner-*.test.ts` reusing the exact `vi.mock("node:child_process", ...)` stub pattern (tests/build-runner-touched-crates.test.ts:33-37), asserting argv via `spawn.mock.calls[0]?.[1]`. Cover: (a) dir→prefixed-name resolution against a stubbed `cargo metadata` JSON (manifest_path in a nested subdir); (b) metadata-failure/empty/non-match → dir-name fallback; (c) cache hit asserts the metadata spawn fires once even when resolveCargoPackageNames is called twice for the same cwd; (d) end-to-end `scopedCargoArgs('test', resolveCargoPackageNames(...), ['--quiet'])` yields `-p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` for the stockfan dir shape. Assert the mock receives `cargo metadata` with discrete argv (named 'cargo', no `shell:true`) to mirror SCENARIO-020/014 conventions.

data.title: Cargo Package Name Resolution for the Scope-Aware Build Gate
data.summary: Fix the scope-aware cargo build-gate in this TypeScript pi-extension so it resolves REAL cargo package names from `cargo metadata` instead of workspace DIRECTORY names. Today `detectTouchedCargoPackages` (src/build-runner.ts) runs `git diff --name-only`, maps each `crates/<seg>/` path to its directory segment, and returns those segments verbatim. `scopedCargoArgs` then emits `-p <dir>`. On any prefixed-crate workspace (stockfan: dirs `data/tools/workflows` → packages `stockfan-data/stockfan-tools/stockfan-workflows`) the gate runs `cargo build -p data -p tools --quiet`, cargo rejects it with `package ID specification 'data' did not match any packages` (exit 101), and the run false-fails before compiling anything — the repair loop cannot fix a framework-derived command. The fix adds `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` (spawns cached `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml`, maps each touched dir to the workspace package whose `manifest_path` parent's first `crates/<seg>/` segment matches, never throws, identity-falls-back to directory names, memoized per-cwd), wires it as the FINAL mapping step inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, guarantees the complete touched set (e2e crate `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` → `stockfan-workflows` is included), and strengthens `buildImplementPrompt` + `buildQaPrompt` (src/prompts.ts) to forbid `--lib`-only self-verification and require full `cargo test -p <pkg>` + spec-mandated e2e. Pure TS change: only src/build-runner.ts, src/prompts.ts, and new/updated tests. nodes.ts/workflow.ts/pipeline.ts/render/theme are untouched. Backward compatible: dir==name workspaces, non-cargo repos, and non-git dirs are byte-identical to today (metadata tier runs only when `language==='rust'` and a non-empty scope resolves). No new runtime deps; the only new spawned process is cached `cargo metadata --no-deps`.
data.architecture:
The change is localized to two source files and a new test file, structured as a four-phase DAG where Phase 3 (prompt discipline) is fully parallelizable with Phases 1–2 (resolver + wiring) because it touches no shared code.

INPUTS / CONTRACTS (all signatures frozen):
- `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd: string, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]` (NEW, exported from src/build-runner.ts). Pure-mapping over a cached side-effect. Given touched DIRECTORY segments (e.g. `["data","tools","workflows"]`), returns REAL package names (e.g. `["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows"]`), deduped, first-seen order preserved.
- `loadCargoMetadata(cwd: string): CargoMetadataResult` (NEW, private). Side-effecting spawn, memoized. `CargoMetadataResult = { ok: true; packages: Array<{ name: string; manifestDir: string }> } | { ok: false }`.
- `cargoMetadataCache: Map<string, CargoMetadataResult>` (NEW, module-level, process-local). Keyed by absolute `cwd`. Stores either the parsed package list OR a `{ ok:false }` failure sentinel so a failing/missing `cargo` is not re-spawned within one run. Never persisted (process exit clears it) → no stale results across runs (SCENARIO-006).
- `detectTouchedCargoPackages(cwd: string, baseRef?: string): string[]` (MODIFIED). Signature unchanged. Internally unchanged: `git -C <cwd> diff --merge-base <ref> --name-only` discrete-argv spawn (no `shell:true`), regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` (first `crates/<seg>/` segment wins), `dedupePreservingOrder`. ONLY addition: the deduped directory segments are passed through `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the FINAL step before return.
- `scopedCargoArgs`, `scopedCargoBuildArgs`, `scopedCargoTestArgs`, `scopedCargoClippyArgs`, `classifyOutOfScopeErrors`, `runBuildGate` (UNCHANGED). `runBuildGate`'s FOUR-tier precedence (opts.testPackages > SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES > detectTouchedCargoPackages > workspace-wide) is untouched — because the resolver is wired INSIDE `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, real names flow through every tier unchanged.

FIX 1 — METADATA RESOLUTION + CACHE (Phase 1):
`loadCargoMetadata(cwd)` spawns `spawnSync("cargo", ["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps","--manifest-path", join(cwd,"Cargo.toml")], { encoding:"utf8", timeout: resolveTimeoutMs() })` — discrete argv, no `shell:true`, same timeout envelope as the rest of the module (AC-10: only new spawn is this one). The `--manifest-path` points at the WORKSPACE-ROOT `Cargo.toml`; cargo returns ALL workspace members in `packages[]`, each carrying its individual `manifest_path`. The helper maps each to `{ name: pkg.name, manifestDir: dirname(pkg.manifest_path) }` and caches it. The whole body is wrapped in a defensive try/catch: spawn error (`r.error`), non-zero exit, timeout, missing `cargo`, JSON parse failure, or unexpected shape → cache `{ ok:false }` and return it. NEVER throws (AC-02, SCENARIO-003).

`resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` reads `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)`. If `ok:false` → return `dedupePreservingOrder(touchedDirs)` (identity fallback, AC-02). If `ok:true` → for each touched segment `d`, apply the SAME `crates/<seg>/` regex to each package's `manifestDir` and select the package whose first `crates/<seg>/` segment EQUALS `d`. This matching rule is what makes manifest-in-subdir work (SCENARIO-002): a package whose `Cargo.toml` lives at `crates/data/inner/Cargo.toml` has `manifestDir = ".../crates/data/inner"` whose first `crates/<seg>/` segment is `data`, so it matches touched segment `data` and resolves to its real `name`. Per-element identity fallback: a touched segment with no matching package degrades to its own directory name (SCENARIO-004). Result is deduped, first-seen order preserved. The fallback chain + never-throw contract is documented in a JSDoc comment (AC-02).

FIX 1 WIRING + FIX 2 — COMPLETE TOUCHED SET (Phase 2):
Inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, the existing `dedupePreservingOrder(pkgs)` call now feeds `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, deduped)` and that result is returned. Because the resolver is identity for dir==name and identity-on-failure, non-cargo/non-git/dir==name paths are byte-identical to today (AC-08). The existing regex already captures the `workflows` segment for `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs`, so Fix 2 is verified (not rewritten): confirm extraction includes data, tools, AND workflows, then the resolver maps `workflows` → `stockfan-workflows` so spec-mandated e2e runs via `cargo test -p stockfan-workflows` (AC-05, SCENARIO-009). End-to-end, `scopedCargoBuildArgs(["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows"])` → `["cargo","build","-p","stockfan-data","-p","stockfan-tools","-p","stockfan-workflows","--quiet"]` (AC-04, SCENARIO-007).

FIX 3 — PROMPT SELF-VERIFY DISCIPLINE (Phase 3, parallelizable):
The authoritative prompt text lives in src/prompts.ts. `buildImplementPrompt`'s "## Instructions" array and `buildQaPrompt`'s "## Instructions" array each get an appended, language-scoped instruction: "When verifying a Rust crate, run `cargo test -p <pkg>` WITHOUT `--lib` so the integration binaries under `tests/` execute, PLUS any spec-mandated e2e/integration target. Do NOT declare green on `--lib`-only evidence — `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries." This is prompt-TEXT only; src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged, so there is no control-flow, nodes, workflow, or pipeline change (AC-07, SCENARIO-010/011). The phrasing is generic ("when Rust") so non-rust stacks are unaffected.

REGRESSION GUARDRAILS:
- The metadata tier runs ONLY when `runBuildGate`'s tier-3 path executes (rust repo, no higher-tier override) — identical to today's `detectTouchedCargoPackages` invocation site. Non-rust stacks never spawn `cargo metadata`.
- Theme method-binding preserved (AC-09, SCENARIO-015/024): no new rendering is introduced; if any were, it MUST use method-style `theme.fg(...)` or a wrapper `const fg=(c,t)=>theme.fg(c,t)` — NEVER `const fg = theme.fg` (detaches `this`, throws "reading 'fgColors'"). tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts stays green.

FILE INVENTORY:
- MODIFY: src/build-runner.ts — add `CargoMetadataResult` type, `cargoMetadataCache`, `loadCargoMetadata`, `resolveCargoPackageNames`; wire final mapping into `detectTouchedCargoPackages`.
- MODIFY: src/prompts.ts — append rust self-verify discipline to `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt`.
- CREATE: test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts — hermetic unit tests (AC-06) with `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked.
- NO CHANGE: src/stages/implementation.ts, src/stages/verify.ts (consume prompts unchanged), nodes.ts, workflow.ts, pipeline.ts, src/render/*, theme, package.json (no new deps).

DATA FLOW (single gate run): git diff → `crates/<dir>/` segments → dedupe → `resolveCargoPackageNames` (cache check → `cargo metadata` if cold → manifestDir-segment match → per-element identity fallback) → real package names → `scopedCargo{Build,Test,Clippy}Args` → `runBuildGate` exec → `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` (now classifies against REAL names, so in-scope partitioning is correct).
data.testingStrategy:
HERMETIC UNIT TESTS (AC-06, SCENARIO-016) in a NEW file test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts, with `node:child_process.spawnSync` mocked via `vi.mock`/module replacement so no real `cargo`/`git` is spawned:

(a) DIR→NAME RESOLUTION: stub `spawnSync` to return a `cargo metadata` JSON whose `packages[]` carry prefixed names (`stockfan-data`, `stockfan-tools`, `stockfan-workflows`) AND one manifest-in-subdir package (`crates/data/inner/Cargo.toml`). Assert `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, ["data","tools","workflows","inner"])` returns `["stockfan-data","stockfan-tools","stockfan-workflows", <inner-pkg-name>]` (AC-01, SCENARIO-001/002/017).
(b) FAILURE FALLBACK: make the mock throw / return `status:101` / emulate timeout (`error` with `killed`/`signal`) / return malformed JSON. For each, assert `resolveCargoPackageNames` returns the input directory names verbatim (identity), and NEVER throws (AC-02, SCENARIO-003/004/018/019). Also assert a touched dir with no matching package falls back to its own name.
(c) CACHE HIT: assert the spawn mock is invoked EXACTLY ONCE across two `resolveCargoPackageNames` calls for the same cwd (record call count on the mock) (AC-03, SCENARIO-005/020).
(d) END-TO-END -p FLAGS: build a workspace where dir≠name and assert `scopedCargoBuildArgs`/`scopedCargoTestArgs`/`scopedCargoClippyArgs` over the resolved set yield `["cargo","build","-p","stockfan-data","-p","stockfan-tools","-p","stockfan-workflows","--quiet"]` and the matching test/clippy argv (AC-04, SCENARIO-007/021/022).
(e) COMPLETE TOUCHED SET: feed a diff containing `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_smoke.rs` and assert `stockfan-workflows` appears in the resolved scope (AC-05, SCENARIO-009).
(f) PROMPT DISCIPLINE: assert `buildImplementPrompt(...)` and `buildQaPrompt(...)` output strings CONTAIN the substrings `cargo test -p`, `WITHOUT --lib` / `no --lib`, `tests/` integration binaries, and `--lib`-only … not … green (AC-07, SCENARIO-010/011).

REGRESSION SUITE (AC-08, SCENARIO-012/013/023) — run the EXISTING suite unchanged and assert all green: build-runner-touched-crates, build-runner-autoscope, build-runner-inscope-classification, build-runner-scoped-args, build-runner-packages, build-runner-nonregression, build-runner-timeout, build-runner-docs, build-runner.test.ts. The key backward-compat invariants to assert inside/alongside these: (1) a dir==name workspace resolves identically (metadata is an identity no-op); (2) non-cargo repos (go/python/node/mixed) and non-git dirs are byte-identical (the metadata spawn is SKIPPED because `detectTouchedCargoPackages` returns `[]` for non-crate / non-git paths before any metadata call, and `runBuildGate` only enters the rust scoping tier when `language==='rust'`); (3) `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` now partitions against REAL names (a `stockfan-data` failure is in-scope when `stockfan-data` is scoped, where previously the `data` directory name never matched the real failure name).

THEME GUARDRAIL (AC-09, SCENARIO-015/024): run tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts; confirm no new rendering was added and any hypothetical rendering uses method-style `theme.fg(...)` (never destructured).

GATE VERIFICATION (AC-10, SCENARIO-014): `npm run typecheck` (strict-clean) and `npm test` (existing + new) both green; `grep` package.json to confirm no new runtime dependency was added; confirm the only new spawned process in build-runner.ts is the cached `cargo metadata --no-deps --manifest-path ...`.

MANUAL SANITY (not a test, evidence-only): on a real prefixed-crate workspace, `detectTouchedCargoPackages` now prints `stockfan-data stockfan-tools stockfan-workflows` and `cargo build -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows --quiet` compiles instead of exiting 101.

OUT OF SCOPE for testing: changing the scoped-vs-workspace-wide gate STRATEGY, baseline-diff scoping, any render/theme token, control-flow in nodes/workflow/pipeline.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Metadata Resolver & Per-Cwd Cache
phases.description: Independently testable: ship `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs)` + private `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)` + module-level `cargoMetadataCache` in src/build-runner.ts. Contract: spawns `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps --manifest-path <cwd>/Cargo.toml` via discrete-argv spawnSync (no shell:true) under the existing `resolveTimeoutMs()` envelope, parses JSON, maps each workspace member to `{name, manifestDir: dirname(manifest_path)}`, caches the result (or a failure sentinel). `resolveCargoPackageNames` maps each touched segment to the package whose manifestDir first `crates/<seg>/` segment equals the touched segment (manifest-in-subdir safe), per-element identity fallback for unmatched dirs, whole-list identity fallback + never-throw on any failure, deduped first-seen order. Covers AC-01/02/03, SCENARIO-001/002/003/004/005/006/017/018/019/020. Unit-tested in isolation with spawnSync mocked (no git, no real cargo).

phases.name: Phase 2 — Wire Resolver Into detectTouchedCargoPackages + Complete Touched Set
phases.description: Depends on Phase 1. Independently testable: inside `detectTouchedCargoPackages`, keep the git-diff discrete-argv spawn, the `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` regex, and `dedupePreservingOrder` byte-identical, then pass the deduped directory segments through `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, dirs)` as the FINAL mapping step before return. Signature unchanged. Verify the regex already captures the `workflows` segment for `crates/workflows/tests/e2e_*.rs` so the e2e crate is not dropped (Fix 2 — verify, not rewrite). End-to-end assertion: a dir≠name workspace produces `cargo build/test/clippy -p stockfan-data -p stockfan-tools -p stockfan-workflows` via the unchanged scopedCargo* builders. Covers AC-04/05, SCENARIO-007/008/009/021/022.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Agent Self-Verification Prompt Discipline (Fix 3)
phases.description: PARALLELIZABLE with Phases 1–2 (touches only src/prompts.ts, shares no code with the resolver). Independently testable: append language-scoped rust verification discipline to `buildImplementPrompt` and `buildQaPrompt` instruction arrays in src/prompts.ts — require `cargo test -p <pkg>` with NO `--lib` flag (so `tests/` integration binaries run), PLUS any spec-mandated e2e/integration target, and an explicit instruction that `--lib`-only evidence is NOT a green because `--lib` skips the `tests/` integration binaries. Prompt-TEXT only: src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged (no control-flow / nodes / workflow / pipeline change). Covers AC-07, SCENARIO-010/011.

phases.name: Phase 4 — Regression Suite, Backward-Compat & Gate Verification
phases.description: Depends on Phases 1–3. Independently testable: run the full existing build-runner test suite (touched-crates, autoscope, inscope-classification, scoped-args, packages, nonregression, timeout, docs, base) and assert green unchanged; confirm dir==name workspaces, non-cargo repos (go/python/node/mixed), and non-git dirs are byte-identical (metadata tier runs only when language==='rust' and a non-empty scope resolves); confirm `classifyOutOfScopeErrors` now partitions against REAL names; run tests/stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts (AC-09 method-binding preserved, no new rendering); `npm run typecheck` strict-clean; `npm test` green (existing + new); assert no new runtime deps and that the only new spawned process is cached `cargo metadata --no-deps`. Covers AC-06/08/09/10, SCENARIO-012/013/014/015/016/023/024.

tasks.description: In src/build-runner.ts add the `CargoMetadataResult` type: `{ ok: true; packages: Array<{ name: string; manifestDir: string }> } | { ok: false }`.
Add module-level `const cargoMetadataCache = new Map<string, CargoMetadataResult>();` (process-local, never persisted — cleared on process exit).

Implement private `loadCargoMetadata(cwd): CargoMetadataResult`: cache check first; else `spawnSync("cargo", ["metadata","--format-version","1","--no-deps","--manifest-path", join(cwd,"Cargo.toml")], { encoding:"utf8", timeout: resolveTimeoutMs() })`; on `r.error || r.status!==0 || non-string stdout` → cache+return `{ok:false}`; else `JSON.parse`, validate `Array.isArray(packages)`, map to `{name, manifestDir: dirname(manifest_path)}`, cache+return `{ok:true,packages}`; whole body try/caught → `{ok:false}`, never throws.

Implement + export `resolveCargoPackageNames(cwd, touchedDirs: string[]): string[]`: read `loadCargoMetadata(cwd)`; if `ok:false` return `dedupePreservingOrder(touchedDirs)`; else for each touched segment `d`, find the package whose `manifestDir` first `crates/<seg>/` segment (regex `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//`) equals `d` and push `pkg.name`, else push `d` (per-element identity fallback); dedupe preserving first-seen order; return. Add JSDoc documenting the never-throw + identity-fallback chain (AC-02).

Create test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts with `spawnSync` mocked: (a) prefixed-name + manifest-in-subdir dir→name resolution; (b) spawn-error / status≠0 / timeout / malformed-JSON → identity fallback + never-throw; (c) cache-hit (spawn called once for two calls same cwd); (d) unmatched touched dir → own name.

data.title: Spec Review — Cargo Package Name Resolution for the Scope-Aware Build Gate
data.summary: The spec is technically sharp and well-grounded for the resolver core (Phase 1–2): every cited symbol in src/build-runner.ts — detectTouchedCargoPackages, the `/(?:^|\/)crates\/([^/]+)\//` regex, dedupePreservingOrder, resolveTimeoutMs, scopedCargo{Args,Build,Test,Clippy}Args, classifyOutOfScopeErrors, runBuildGate, and the SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES four-tier precedence — is verified present and matches the described contract. The `cargo metadata --no-deps` design is sound (workspace members are always in packages[]; --no-deps only excludes non-workspace deps), the discrete-argv no-shell spawn is consistent with module conventions, and AC-01..AC-10 plus SCENARIO-001..024 are all mapped. However, two grounding defects block approval as-written. (1) CRITICAL: the spec's Fix 3 appends rust self-verify discipline to buildQaPrompt and asserts "src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged" — but grep confirms buildQaPrompt has ZERO call sites in src/ or tests/ (verify.ts imports buildCodeReviewPrompt/buildAdversarialReview/buildFixPrompt/buildApiTestPrompt/buildUiTestPrompt, never buildQaPrompt). Appending to it is a no-op; the QA/verify agent — exactly where the "no --lib-only green" rule matters most — never receives the instruction. The string-containment test (Testing Strategy item f) would pass while delivering false confidence, matching the repo's known "implemented-and-unit-tested-but-NOT-wired-in" failure mode (learned.md score:62). (2) HIGH: the spec says CREATE test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts, but the repo has NO test/ directory — vitest.config.ts globs only tests/** and src/**. A literal implementation creates a file vitest never discovers, so AC-06/AC-10 can false-green. Both are bounded, recoverable reroutes/renames; the resolver architecture itself is APPROVED-quality.

findings.title: buildQaPrompt is dead code (0 call sites) — Fix 3 QA-path discipline will never reach an agent
findings.detail: Architecture (Fix 3) and FILE INVENTORY claim: "buildImplementPrompt's and buildQaPrompt's '## Instructions' array each get an appended instruction" and "src/stages/implementation.ts and src/stages/verify.ts consume these builders unchanged." Grounding check: grep -rn buildQaPrompt across src/ and tests/ returns ONLY its definition at src/prompts.ts:97 — no importer. src/stages/verify.ts:15 imports buildCodeReviewPrompt, buildAdversarialPrompt, buildFixPrompt, buildApiTestPrompt, buildUiTestPrompt (NOT buildQaPrompt); src/stages/implementation.ts:10 imports buildTddPrompt, buildImplementPrompt, buildCommitPrompt, buildImplementationSummaryPrompt (NOT buildQaPrompt). Consequence: appending rust verify discipline to buildQaPrompt has zero runtime effect; the verify/QA agent never sees "do NOT declare green on --lib-only evidence." AC-07 / SCENARIO-010/011 are only half-satisfiable (implementer path works via buildImplementPrompt; QA path is hollow). Testing Strategy item (f) asserts the substring exists in buildQaPrompt output — that test passes while proving nothing about agent behavior. This is the exact "tested-but-not-wired-in" anti-pattern in learned.md (score:62). Recommendation: reroute the QA-path discipline to a prompt verify.ts actually consumes (buildFixPrompt is the implementer-driven fix entry; buildCodeReviewPrompt is the reviewer entry) OR delete buildQaPrompt and restate AC-07 as implementer-path-only. Do not leave the spec claiming buildQaPrompt is live.

findings.title: New test path test/ does not exist — vitest will not discover the file (AC-06/AC-10 false-green risk)
findings.detail: FILE INVENTORY: "CREATE: test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts" and Testing Strategy repeatedly references test/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts. Grounding check: find . -type d -name test (excluding node_modules) returns nothing; the repo uses tests/ (plural). vitest.config.ts: include: ["tests/**/*.test.ts", "src/**/*.test.ts"]. A file created at test/... is outside both globs and will NOT run under `npm test`. If the implementer follows the spec literally, AC-06's hermetic unit tests never execute yet `npm test` stays green (AC-10) — false confidence in the resolver. Recommendation: change every occurrence to tests/build-runner-package-resolution.test.ts to match the established convention.

findings.title: "Language-scoped" prompt append bypasses the existing languageInstructions mechanism and isn't actually scoped
findings.detail: Fix 3 calls the appended instruction "language-scoped," but buildQaPrompt(s,c,phase) has no language parameter and buildImplementPrompt already receives rust-specific text via specialist.languageInstructions (src/prompts.ts:94; sourced from loadLangProfile in src/helpers.ts:78). The spec instead hardcodes rust text into the generic builder, so it is emitted in EVERY prompt regardless of classified language; only the runtime phrasing ("when verifying a Rust crate") gates behavior. This couples rust knowledge into the wrong layer and duplicates the lang-profile channel. Recommendation: either route the discipline through the rust lang-profile (loadLangProfile) so it is truly language-scoped and reaches buildImplementPrompt via the existing li block, or drop the "language-scoped" wording and explicitly state the text is unconditionally present but runtime-conditional.

findings.title: cargoMetadataCache key normalization unspecified — risks redundant spawns and weakens AC-03 "exactly once"
findings.detail: Architecture states cargoMetadataCache is "Keyed by absolute cwd." But neither loadCargoMetadata nor resolveCargoPackageNames is specified to canonicalize the key (e.g., path.resolve(cwd) or fs.realpathSync). detectTouchedCargoPackages receives cwd as-is from runBuildGate(opts.cwd); if a caller passes a relative path, a trailing slash, or a symlink variant, cache keys diverge and cargo metadata is re-spawned — directly weakening AC-03 / SCENARIO-005 ("spawn invoked EXACTLY ONCE") and the no-re-spawn-on-failure sentinel guarantee. Recommendation: mandate key normalization (resolve(cwd)) in the loadCargoMetadata contract and add a cache-key-normalization assertion to test (c).

findings.title: Regression-suite reference to "build-runner.test.ts" is ambiguous — two such files exist
findings.detail: Testing Strategy / regression suite lists "build-runner.test.ts" alongside the build-runner-* files. Grounding check: TWO files match — src/build-runner.test.ts AND tests/build-runner.test.ts (both covered by vitest's include globs). The spec does not disambiguate which must stay green. Low impact (both will run regardless), but for a backward-compat assertion that hinges on byte-identical behavior the target should be explicit. Recommendation: name both paths explicitly in the regression list.

dimensions.name: D1 Completeness
dimensions.notes: All 10 ACs (AC-01..AC-10 verified in 01-requirements.md) are addressed; SCENARIO-001..024 each mapped to a phase/spec section; error paths (never-throw, identity fallback, {ok:false} sentinel) and NFRs (no new deps, cached spawn) explicit. No uncovered AC.

dimensions.name: D2 Consistency
dimensions.notes: Symbol names, signatures, regex, env var (SUPER_DEV_BUILD_TEST_PACKAGES), and tier precedence match across Summary/Architecture/Testing/Phases. scopedCargo* argv construction verified against src/build-runner.ts.

dimensions.name: D3 Feasibility
dimensions.notes: cargo metadata --no-deps correctly returns workspace members in packages[]; discrete-argv spawnSync (no shell:true) matches module convention; identity fallback makes the resolver a safe drop-in. Architecture fits existing patterns.

dimensions.name: D4 Testability
dimensions.notes: Hermetic spawnSync-mock strategy is concrete and numeric ("exactly once," substring assertions). Concern: cache-key normalization (F-04) and the buildQaPrompt string test (F-01) can both produce green-but-hollow results.

dimensions.name: D5 Traceability
dimensions.notes: AC-07→SCENARIO-010/011 traceability chain is BROKEN on the QA path: buildQaPrompt is never consumed by any stage (F-01), so the spec's claim that verify.ts consumes the builder is false. implementer-path traceability is intact.

dimensions.name: D6 Grounding
dimensions.notes: Score ≈ 18/20 verified (~90%) but the 2 misses are material: buildQaPrompt dead-code wiring claim (F-01) and test/ vs tests/ path (F-02). All build-runner.ts symbols, env vars, regex, regression files, and stream-theme-class-theme.test.ts verified present.

dimensions.name: D7 Complexity
dimensions.notes: File inventory is minimal and proportional (2 source files + 1 test). Concern (F-03): hardcoding rust text into generic prompt builders duplicates the existing loadLangProfile/languageInstructions channel rather than reusing it.

dimensions.name: D8 Ambiguity
dimensions.notes: Type contracts (CargoMetadataResult), fallback chain, and matching rule are explicit. Concerns: cache-key normalization unspecified (F-04); "language-scoped" mis-describes an unconditional append (F-03); "build-runner.test.ts" target ambiguous (F-05).
